product: Cisco Meraki Dashboard API v1 — MX L3 firewall rules

auth.note: required on every request; never emitted in error text

success.body: {rules: [{comment, policy, protocol, srcPort, srcCidr, destPort, destCidr, syslogEnabled}]}
success.note: the live dashboard appends the special default rule (comment 'Default rule', allow any/any) as the FINAL entry of the returned list; reconcilers must ignore it when comparing

body.rules: ordered array replacing the ENTIRE rule list (full replacement, not append); must NOT include the special default rule
body.syslogDefaultRule: optional boolean, logs the special default rule; omitted from the body unless the caller sets it

rule_schema.comment: optional description; normalized default is the empty string
rule_schema.srcPort: comma-separated ports 1-65535 or 'any'; optional, defaults to any
rule_schema.destPort: comma-separated ports 1-65535 or 'any'; optional, defaults to any

normalization.any_literal: the dashboard renders the wildcard as 'Any'; clients compare case-insensitively by lowercasing the literal any (other values keep their exact spelling)
normalization.ordering: rules are an ORDERED list; the same rules in a different order are drift
normalization.no_op: when normalized desired equals normalized current (default rule excluded), no PUT is issued

mock_fixture.server_error: At least one of your firewall rules is invalid
